hypothesis: Incorporating protein conformational ensemble-derived pharmacophore consensus models—rather than a single static pharmacophore derived from known ligands—as the reference set within the CATS-guided reward function will yield generated molecules with significantly improved binding affinity and reduced off-target activity compared to single-conformation pharmacophore guidance, because ensemble-based pharmacophores capture the dynamic flexibility of the alpha estrogen receptor binding site and thereby encode a richer set of interaction constraints that better discriminate true binders from decoys during reinforcement learning optimization.
